notes.anchor: he wants the Senate to attend in numbers on the Kalends
notes.note: Caesar summoned the Senate to meet at Rome on the Kalends (1st) of April 49 BC to give a constitutional veneer to his position; Cicero, dreading what he would have to say there, agonizes over whether to obey the proclamation.

notes.anchor: give my Cicero his white toga
notes.note: The toga virilis (or toga pura), the plain white toga assumed by a Roman boy at coming of age, replacing the purple-bordered toga praetexta of childhood. Cicero performs this rite for his son Marcus, then about sixteen, at Arpinum since Rome is closed to them.

Arpinum, the hill-town in the Volscian country about 100 km southeast of Rome, was Cicero's birthplace and ancestral home; with the capital barred to him he falls back on it both for refuge and for his son's coming-of-age ceremony.

notes.anchor: anything has been written to you about Tiro
notes.note: Marcus Tullius Tiro, Cicero's learned freedman and confidential secretary, had fallen ill and been left behind on the journey; Cicero, anxious for news, prizes his service and loyalty especially in the present crisis.
